description.content: Operations for managing YouTube live broadcast events
Returns a list of YouTube broadcasts that match the API request parameters. You can retrieve broadcasts for the authenticated user or retrieve specific broadcasts by their IDs.

query.description: (Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.
Comma-separated list of YouTube broadcast IDs for the resources being retrieved.
Set this parameter to true to instruct the API to return only broadcasts owned by the authenticated user.
Filters the API response to only include broadcasts with the specified status.
Filters results by the type of broadcast. Acceptable values are event and persistent.
The maximum number of items that should be returned in the result set. Acceptable values are 0 to 50, inclusive.
Identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. The nextPageToken property in the API response identifies other pages that are available.
Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use this parameter to reduce bandwidth usage by selecting only the fields you need.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

response.name: Successful response containing a list of live broadcast resources.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2
Creates a broadcast. The authenticated user must be authorized to create broadcasts. After creating the broadcast, you will need to bind it to a stream and set it live.

query.description: (Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.
Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use this parameter to reduce bandwidth usage by selecting only the fields you need.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

response.name: Successful response containing the newly created live broadcast resource.

description.content: Deletes a broadcast. The authenticated user must own the broadcast being deleted. Broadcasts cannot be deleted while they are in the live or testing status.

query.description: (Required) The ID of the broadcast to delete.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

response.name: The broadcast was successfully deleted.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

Binds a YouTube broadcast to a stream or removes an existing binding between a broadcast and a stream. A broadcast can only be bound to one video stream, though a video stream may be bound to more than one broadcast.

query.description: (Required) The id parameter specifies the unique ID of the broadcast that is being bound to a video stream.
(Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.

The streamId parameter specifies the unique ID of the video stream that is being bound to a broadcast. If this parameter is omitted, the API will remove any existing binding between the broadcast and a video stream.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

response.name: Successful response containing the updated live broadcast resource.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

Changes the status of a YouTube live broadcast and initiates any processes associated with the new status. Valid transitions are: ready -> testing, testing -> live, live -> complete, ready -> live (skips testing state).

query.description: (Required) The id parameter specifies the unique ID of the broadcast that is transitioning to another status.
(Required) The broadcastStatus parameter identifies the state to which the broadcast is changing.
(Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

response.name: Successful response containing the updated live broadcast resource.

Operations for managing messages in YouTube live chat
Lists live chat messages for a specific live broadcast. This method does not support filtering by user. The list is ordered chronologically, starting with the earliest message.

query.description: (Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.

(Required) The liveChatId parameter specifies the ID of the live chat whose messages will be returned. The live chat ID associated with a broadcast is returned in the liveBroadcast resource's snippet.liveChatId property.
The maximum number of items that should be returned in the result set. Acceptable values are 0 to 50, inclusive.
Identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. The nextPageToken property in the API response identifies other pages that are available.
Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use this parameter to reduce bandwidth usage by selecting only the fields you need.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

item.name: Youtube Send a Live Chat Message

request.name: Youtube Send a Live Chat Message

description.content: Adds a message to a live chat. The authenticated user must be authorized to participate in the chat. Some chat types may have restrictions on who can send messages.

Operations for managing moderators in YouTube live chat
Lists moderators for a live chat. This list is ordered by date added, with the most recently added moderator first.

query.description: (Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.
(Required) The liveChatId parameter specifies the YouTube live chat for which the API should return moderators.
The maximum number of items that should be returned in the result set. Acceptable values are 0 to 50, inclusive.
Identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. The nextPageToken property in the API response identifies other pages that are available.
Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use this parameter to reduce bandwidth usage by selecting only the fields you need.

Operations for managing YouTube live video streams
Returns a list of video streams that match the API request parameters. You can retrieve streams for the authenticated user or retrieve specific streams by their IDs.

query.description: (Required) Specifies a comma-separated list of one or more resource properties that the API response will include. The part parameter value must include the id property.
Comma-separated list of live stream IDs for the resources being retrieved.
Set this parameter to true to instruct the API to return only streams owned by the authenticated user.
The maximum number of items that should be returned in the result set. Acceptable values are 0 to 50, inclusive.
Identifies a specific page in the result set that should be returned. The nextPageToken property in the API response identifies other pages that are available.
Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response. Use this parameter to reduce bandwidth usage by selecting only the fields you need.
API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

The YouTube Live Streaming API lets you create, update, and manage live video broadcasts and live video streams on YouTube. The API provides resources for liveBroadcasts, liveStreams, liveChatMessages, liveChatModerators, liveChatBans, and superChatEvents. Broadcasts are scheduled events that viewers can watch live, while streams are the actual video feed bound to a broadcast.
